brand_dna.category: British sporting-heritage menswear and lifestyle apparel, sizes S to 6XL, sold direct online.
brand_dna.core_belief: We build for those who do the heavy lifting - where the integrity of the stitch meets the grit of the graft. No shortcuts. No disposable style.
proof_points: Founded in 2007 by Rugby World Cup winner Phil Vickery
Designed in the Cotswolds, UK-based customer support team
100% organic cotton, double stitched for extra strength (core tees and polos)
90 days hassle-free returns, free UK returns
brand_dna.positioning: Rugged, value-driven premium: built-to-last quality with honest pricing, not fast fashion and not luxury.

voice_pillars.name: Grit And Graft
voice_pillars.desc: Plain-spoken, hard-working tone that speaks to men who get things done and want kit that keeps up.
voice_pillars.quote: We build for those who do the heavy lifting.

voice_pillars.name: Built To Last
voice_pillars.desc: Every line leans on durability and craftsmanship over trend-chasing or throwaway style.
voice_pillars.quote: No shortcuts. No disposable style. Just kit built to last.

voice_pillars.name: Blunt And Confident
voice_pillars.desc: Short, punchy statements that assume the quality speaks for itself without over-explaining.

voice_pillars.name: For Real Bodies
voice_pillars.desc: Down-to-earth honesty about fit that puts the wearer, not the runway, first.

phrases: No shortcuts on the stitch, just more for your money.
made to get better with every wear

icp: Practical UK men, often 35+ and across a wider size range (S to 6XL), who value durable, no-nonsense clothing and will pay a fair price for kit that lasts rather than chasing trends.

personas.name: The Grafter
personas.description: Hard-working bloke who wants clothes that hold up to real use - move him with durability, double stitching and built-to-last messaging.

personas.name: The Rugby Loyalist
personas.description: Fan of the brand's sporting heritage and Phil Vickery pedigree - move him with the founder story, rugby roots and British-made-in-the-Cotswolds credibility.

personas.name: The Fuller-Fit Shopper
personas.description: Man who struggles to find good clothing in larger sizes - move him with the S to 6XL range, generous fit and 'made for men, not mannequins' honesty.

products_ladder.title: Army Rugby Union Long Sleeve Rugby - Red

offers: Free UK delivery on orders over £100 (standard £3.95 otherwise)
90 days hassle-free returns, free within the UK
Multi-buy deals: 3 for 2 Polo Shirts, 3 for 2 Organic T-Shirts, 2 Summer Shorts for £100
Win a £500 Raging Bull gift card promotion

allowed.claim: Founded in 2007 by Rugby World Cup winner Phil Vickery.
100% organic cotton, double stitched for extra strength (core tees and polos).
Designed in the Cotswolds with a UK-based customer support team.
90 days hassle-free returns, free within the UK.
Free UK delivery on orders over £100.

banned.claim: Calling the range 'sustainable' or 'eco-friendly' on the basis of organic cotton alone.
banned.why: FTC Green Guides - unqualified sustainability claim not backed by specific, verifiable proof.

banned.claim: '100% recycled' or 'made from recycled fabric' for any garment.
banned.why: FTC Green Guides - no recycled-content data is stated on the site; unverifiable claim.
FTC Green Guides - carbon/impact claim with no verifiable backing.

banned.claim: Framing the fit or fabric as relieving back pain, correcting posture or easing joint strain.
'Organic' used to imply broad environmental benefit beyond the cotton material itself.
banned.why: FTC Green Guides - overstates a specific material fact into an unqualified green claim.
